basic_details.job_title: IOCL Barauni Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 432 Technician, Trade Apprentice Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Indian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL), Barauni Refinery
basic_details.application_mode: Online via NAPS/NATS Portal + Microsoft Form
basic_details.short_information: This section gives candidates the core facts about the IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice Recruitment 2026 in one place. It covers the hiring unit, total seats, application mode, and closing date. Use this table as a quick reference before checking detailed eligibility below.

age_limit.details: The minimum age for IOCL Barauni Apprentice 2026 is 18 years, and the maximum is 24 years as on 30 June 2026 for General/EWS candidates. Age relaxation is extended to SC, ST, OBC (NCL), and PwBD candidates as per Government of India guidelines — up to 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), and up to 10 years for PwBD candidates irrespective of reservation. Only the Matriculation (Class X) certificate issued by the Board of Secondary Education is accepted as proof of age.

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: B.Sc (Maths, Physics, Chemistry or Industrial Chemistry)
Matric with ITI in Fitter Trade (min. 2 years, Pass class)
B.Sc (Maths, Physics, Chemistry or Industrial Chemistry)
Class XII pass with Skill Certificate in Domestic Data Entry Operator

qualification.details: Eligibility for IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice Recruitment 2026 depends on the trade applied for, with qualification ranging from Matric with ITI to Graduation and Diploma. Candidates must have already passed the prescribed qualification as on the eligibility cut-off date — those awaiting results are not eligible. The minimum aggregate marks required are 50% for general candidates and 45% for SC/ST/PwBD candidates against reserved seats. | The minimum age for IOCL Barauni Apprentice 2026 is 18 years, and the maximum is 24 years as on 30 June 2026 for General/EWS candidates. Age relaxation is extended to SC, ST, OBC (NCL), and PwBD candidates as per Government of India guidelines — up to 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), and up to 10 years for PwBD candidates irrespective of reservation. Only the Matriculation (Class X) certificate issued by the Board of Secondary Education is accepted as proof of age.

salary_details.stipend: As per Apprentices Act 1961/1973 & Apprentices Rules 1992

salary_details.general leave: 32 days (8 days per quarter, incl. medical grounds)
salary_details.details: The stipend payable to apprentices selected under IOCL Barauni Refinery Recruitment 2026 follows the rates prescribed under the Apprentices Act, 1961/1973, Apprentices Rules 1992, and Corporation guidelines, rather than a fixed flat figure stated in this notification. Apprentices engaged against Codes 101 to 107 receive one pair of safety shoes and a helmet, returnable upon training completion. | Importantly, IOCL has clarified that the Corporation has no obligation to offer regular employment upon completion of the apprenticeship period, and apprentices cannot claim employment rights based solely on completing their training. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

selection_process: Candidates wondering about the IOCL Barauni selection process can note that there is no written test for this apprentice engagement. Selection is based purely on the percentage of marks scored in the prescribed qualification
among candidates registered on the NAPS/NATS portal.

Merit Listing: Candidates are ranked by aggregate percentage of marks (all years/semesters) in the prescribed qualification for their trade. Preference is given to first-time apprenticeship applicants. Tie-Breaker: In case of equal marks
the candidate senior in age (date of birth) is ranked higher; if dates of birth also match

higher Matriculation marks decide the rank. PwBD Consideration: PwBD candidates who avail relaxation in qualifying marks are considered against reserved seats based on merit within their category. Document Verification (Tentative: 11–18 August 2026): Shortlisted candidates are called based on merit list position and seat availability in their trade. Pre-Engagement Medical Fitness: Only candidates declared medically fit as per Apprentices Act standards are considered for final engagement. Offer of Engagement: Issued to candidates whose documents and medical fitness certificate are found in order.
how_to_apply: Candidates can apply for IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice Recruitment 2026 only through the official NAPS/NATS portals along with a mandatory Microsoft Form, accessible via www.iocl.com from 29 June 2026 to 28 July 2026. Both Step I and Step II must be completed for the application to be considered valid.
For Codes 101, 102, 103, 108, 109, 110 & 111: Register on the NAPS portal at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in, search location as IOCL Barauni Refinery, select the trade, and complete registration.
For Codes 104, 105, 106 & 107: Register on the NATS/BOAT portal at nats.education.gov.in, then apply to the IOCL Barauni Refinery advertisement from the dashboard.
Use Establishment ID EBRBEC000003 for NATS or E11191000001 for NAPS while applying.
Verify that personal details, educational details, category, and aggregate percentage are correctly displayed before final submission.
After applying on NAPS/NATS, visit the "Apprenticeship Additional Data Form: Barauni Refinery 2026" link on www.iocl.com.
Fill in details matching exactly with your NAPS/NATS application, including enrolment number and registration number.
Submit only one response — duplicate or incomplete Microsoft Form entries are liable to be rejected.
Use the same email ID and mobile number registered on NAPS/NATS, valid for at least one year.
Candidates can apply for apprenticeship in only one trade/discipline of their choice; applying for more than one trade results in summary rejection of all applications.

content_sections.heading: IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice 2026 Quick Overview
content_sections.text: This section gives candidates the core facts about the IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice Recruitment 2026 in one place. It covers the hiring unit, total seats, application mode, and closing date. Use this table as a quick reference before checking detailed eligibility below.

rows.information: Indian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL), Barauni Refinery
Online via NAPS/NATS Portal + Microsoft Form

content_sections.heading: IOCL Barauni Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the IOCL Barauni Apprentice 2026 timeline can note that the notification was issued on 29 June 2026, with applications accepted on the NAPS/NATS portals from the same date. The eligibility cut-off date for age and qualification is also fixed, and document verification follows shortly after the application window closes.

rows.event: Opening of Online Application (NAPS/NATS + MS Forms)
Last Date for Online Application Submission
Tentative Publication of Document Verification List

content_sections.heading: IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: The IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice Vacancy 2026 covers 11 trade and technician categories totaling 432 seats. The Attendant Operator (Chemical Plant) trade has the highest number of openings at 237, followed by Boiler Trade Apprentice at 39. Candidates should check the post code, qualification, and reserved seats relevant to their trade before applying. PwBD candidates can apply only against identified seats under Codes 102, 105, 106, 107, 108, 109, 110, and 111, since Petroleum Refining is classified as a hazardous process and certain trades are not open to PwBD reservation.

rows.trade_discipline: Trade Apprentice – Data Entry Operator (Skill Certificate Holder)

content_sections.heading: IOCL Barauni Apprentice Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Eligibility for IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice Recruitment 2026 depends on the trade applied for, with qualification ranging from Matric with ITI to Graduation and Diploma. Candidates must have already passed the prescribed qualification as on the eligibility cut-off date — those awaiting results are not eligible. The minimum aggregate marks required are 50% for general candidates and 45% for SC/ST/PwBD candidates against reserved seats. The minimum age for IOCL Barauni Apprentice 2026 is 18 years, and the maximum is 24 years as on 30 June 2026 for General/EWS candidates. Age relaxation is extended to SC, ST, OBC (NCL), and PwBD candidates as per Government of India guidelines — up to 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), and up to 10 years for PwBD candidates irrespective of reservation. Only the Matriculation (Class X) certificate issued by the Board of Secondary Education is accepted as proof of age.

rows.qualification: B.Sc (Maths, Physics, Chemistry or Industrial Chemistry)
Matric with ITI in Fitter Trade (min. 2 years, Pass class)
B.Sc (Maths, Physics, Chemistry or Industrial Chemistry)
Class XII pass with Skill Certificate in Domestic Data Entry Operator

content_sections.heading: IOCL Barauni Apprentice Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the IOCL Barauni selection process can note that there is no written test for this apprentice engagement. Selection is based purely on the percentage of marks scored in the prescribed qualification, in descending order, among candidates registered on the NAPS/NATS portal. Merit Listing: Candidates are ranked by aggregate percentage of marks (all years/semesters) in the prescribed qualification for their trade. Preference is given to first-time apprenticeship applicants. Tie-Breaker: In case of equal marks, the candidate senior in age (date of birth) is ranked higher; if dates of birth also match, higher Matriculation marks decide the rank. PwBD Consideration: PwBD candidates who avail relaxation in qualifying marks are considered against reserved seats based on merit within their category. Document Verification (Tentative: 11–18 August 2026): Shortlisted candidates are called based on merit list position and seat availability in their trade. Pre-Engagement Medical Fitness: Only candidates declared medically fit as per Apprentices Act standards are considered for final engagement. Offer of Engagement: Issued to candidates whose documents and medical fitness certificate are found in order.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for IOCL Barauni Apprentice Recruitment 2026 (Apply Online)
content_sections.text: Candidates can apply for IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice Recruitment 2026 only through the official NAPS/NATS portals along with a mandatory Microsoft Form, accessible via www.iocl.com from 29 June 2026 to 28 July 2026. Both Step I and Step II must be completed for the application to be considered valid. After applying on NAPS/NATS, visit the "Apprenticeship Additional Data Form: Barauni Refinery 2026" link on www.iocl.com. Fill in details matching exactly with your NAPS/NATS application, including enrolment number and registration number. Submit only one response — duplicate or incomplete Microsoft Form entries are liable to be rejected. Use the same email ID and mobile number registered on NAPS/NATS, valid for at least one year. Candidates can apply for apprenticeship in only one trade/discipline of their choice; applying for more than one trade results in summary rejection of all applications.

content_sections.heading: IOCL Barauni Apprentice Stipend & Other Benefits 2026
content_sections.text: The stipend payable to apprentices selected under IOCL Barauni Refinery Recruitment 2026 follows the rates prescribed under the Apprentices Act, 1961/1973, Apprentices Rules 1992, and Corporation guidelines, rather than a fixed flat figure stated in this notification. Apprentices engaged against Codes 101 to 107 receive one pair of safety shoes and a helmet, returnable upon training completion. Importantly, IOCL has clarified that the Corporation has no obligation to offer regular employment upon completion of the apprenticeship period, and apprentices cannot claim employment rights based solely on completing their training. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

rows.details: As per Apprentices Act 1961/1973 & Apprentices Rules 1992
One pair of safety shoes & a Helmet (returnable)
32 days (8 days per quarter, incl. medical grounds)

content_sections.heading: IOCL Barauni Apprentice Document Verification Requirements
content_sections.text: Shortlisted candidates must carry original documents along with self-attested copies at the time of document verification, tentatively scheduled between 11 and 18 August 2026. Missing or incorrect documents at this stage can lead to automatic rejection of candidature. 10th Pass/Matriculation certificate (proof of date of birth) Class XII / ITI / Graduation / Diploma marksheets (semester-wise/year-wise, as applicable) Final qualification certificate issued by the respective Board/Authority/NCVET/SCVT CGPA/OGPA to percentage conversion certificate, if applicable Certificate confirming regular full-time mode of study and good conduct, from the Principal of the institute SC/ST/Disability/OBC(NCL)/EWS Income & Asset Certificate, as applicable, in the prescribed format Skill Certificate (for Code 111 candidates only) Print of NAPS/NATS portal registration with applicant details Affidavit (Annexure-A) confirming compliance with apprenticeship eligibility conditions One original Photo ID proof (Aadhar/PAN/Voter ID/Driving License/Passport) SC/ST/PwBD candidates attending document verification are reimbursed single second-class railway fare from their nearest railway station to the verification venue, provided the distance is 30 KM or more.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying for IOCL Barauni Apprentice 2026
content_sections.text: Many candidates risk rejection due to avoidable errors during the IOCL Barauni Refinery online application process. Reviewing these common mistakes beforehand can help ensure your application is processed smoothly. Skipping Step II: Completing only the NAPS/NATS registration without filling the mandatory Microsoft Form results in an incomplete application — both steps are compulsory. Applying for multiple trades: Candidates applying for more than one Trade/Discipline will have all their applications summarily rejected. Mismatched details across portals: Enrollment number, registration number, and personal details must match exactly between the NAPS/NATS portal and the Microsoft Form. Using an inactive email/mobile number: All future communication happens only via email/SMS/website, so the contact details must remain valid for the entire apprenticeship period. Ignoring CGPA conversion certificates: Candidates with CGPA/OGPA grades must obtain an official percentage conversion certificate from their institute before document verification, or risk rejection. Applying with pending results: Candidates whose qualifying exam result is awaited as on 30 June 2026 are not eligible and should wait until results are declared.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for IOCL Barauni Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application through NAPS/NATS portal and Microsoft Form is 28 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in IOCL Barauni Refinery Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: There are 432 total Apprentice vacancies across 11 trades, including Attendant Operator, Fitter, Boiler, four Technician disciplines, Secretarial Assistant, Accountant, and Data Entry Operator.

faq.question: Q3. Is there an application fee for IOCL Barauni Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No application fee has been mentioned in the official notification for this apprentice engagement drive.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for IOCL Barauni Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and maximum age is 24 years as on 30 June 2026 for General/EWS candidates, with standard relaxation for SC, ST, OBC (NCL), and PwBD candidates.

faq.question: Q5. Is there a written exam for IOCL Barauni Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, there is no written test. Selection is based purely on merit calculated from the percentage of marks obtained in the prescribed qualification.

faq.question: Q6. Can a candidate apply for more than one trade in IOCL Barauni Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: No, a candidate can apply for only one trade/discipline; applications for multiple trades will be summarily rejected.

faq.question: Q7. Does completing the IOCL apprenticeship guarantee a job?
faq.answer: No, IOCL has explicitly stated there is no obligation to offer employment after apprenticeship completion, and apprentices cannot claim employment rights on this basis.

faq.question: Q8. What documents are required for IOCL Barauni Apprentice document verification?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry original Matriculation certificate, qualification marksheets and certificates, category certificates (if applicable), NAPS/NATS registration printout, and a government photo ID.

faq.question: Q9. When will document verification be held for IOCL Barauni Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Document verification is tentatively scheduled between 11 August 2026 and 18 August 2026, with the shortlist published around 5 August 2026.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I check the official IOCL Barauni Apprentice Recruitment 2026 notification?
faq.answer: The official notification and all updates are published only on www.iocl.com under the "What's New" section; candidates should be cautious of fraudulent job postings on social media.
